title: Complete error handling and logging improvements
description:

REMAINING:

- Update tests for new exception types

close_reason: Completed error handling and logging integration. All modules updated with logging and custom exceptions. Tests passing. Documentation added.

title: Make table viewer windows modal (multiple windows)
description:
Change table viewer to open in separate modal windows instead of a single panel, allowing users to:
- Open multiple tables simultaneously
- Compare tables side-by-side
- Each window should be independent and closeable
close_reason:
Implemented modal table viewer windows:
- Created TableViewerWindow class for independent table windows
- Each table opens in its own window (can have multiple open)
- Removed embedded viewer from main window
- Main window now shows table browser only
- Track open windows and clean up closed ones automatically
- Window title shows table name
- Status bar shows count of open table windows
- Allows side-by-side comparison of multiple tables

title: Implement ROM modification tracking and project management
description:
Create a Git-like modification tracking system for ROM edits with project organization.

## Problem Statement
Users currently track ROM modifications manually in Excel spreadsheets. Multiple ROMs often have the same filename (e.g., 'stock.bin'), making organization difficult. Need a built-in system to:
- Track all table modifications with comments
- Organize multiple ROMs under projects with aliases
- View change history and compare versions
- Export modification logs

## Core Features

### 1. Project Management System
**Purpose:** Organize related ROMs under a single project
- Create/open/save projects (.ncproj files)
- Project contains: name, description, list of ROMs
- Each ROM has an alias (user-friendly name like 'Stock', 'Stage 1', 'Final Tune')
- Projects stored as SQLite database or JSON file
- Recent projects list in File menu

### 2. Modification Tracking
**Purpose:** Record every table edit automatically
- Track when table values are changed
- Record: timestamp, table name, address, old value, new value, user comment
- Store baseline (original ROM) for comparison
- Automatic tracking (no manual saving required)
- Optional: attach comments when making changes

### 3. Change History Viewer
**Purpose:** View all modifications made to a ROM
- List all changes chronologically
- Filter by: table name, date range, comment text
- Show before/after values
- Edit/add comments retroactively
- Undo changes (revert to previous value)

### 4. Comparison Tools
**Purpose:** Compare ROMs and track differences
- Compare current ROM to baseline (show what changed)
- Compare two ROMs in the same project (Stock vs Tuned)
- Highlight modified tables in tree view
- Show delta values (e.g., '+5.0', '-10%')
- Visual diff viewer for tables

### 5. Export & Documentation
**Purpose:** Generate modification reports
- Export change log to PDF/text/CSV
- Include: all changes, comments, timestamps
- Useful for documentation or sharing with tuner

## Technical Design

### Data Model


### Storage Options
1. **SQLite Database** (recommended)
   - Single .ncproj file
   - Efficient querying for history/filtering
   - Supports large projects
   
2. **JSON File** (simpler alternative)
   - Human-readable
   - Easier to implement
   - May be slow for large change logs

### Implementation Phases

**Phase 1: Project Structure (Foundation)**
- Create Project data model
- Implement project file format (SQLite schema or JSON structure)
- Add File > New Project, Open Project, Save Project
- Add ROM to project with alias
- Basic project settings dialog

**Phase 2: Change Tracking Backend**
- Modify table_viewer to emit change events
- Create ChangeTracker class to record modifications
- Store baseline ROM data when project is created
- Intercept write_table_data() calls to log changes
- Add comment dialog when saving changes

**Phase 3: Change History UI**
- Create ChangeHistoryWindow to display modifications
- List all changes with filters (date, table, comment)
- Show old value → new value
- Allow editing comments
- Add Tools > Change History menu

**Phase 4: Comparison Features**
- Compare current ROM to baseline (modified tables indicator)
- Compare two ROMs side-by-side
- Add visual indicators in table browser (modified tables)
- Highlight changed cells in table viewer
- Delta calculation and display

**Phase 5: Export & Polish**
- Export change log to PDF/text/CSV
- Add project templates
- Project backup/restore
- Settings: auto-comment frequency, track anonymous changes

## UI Mockups

### Main Window Changes
- Menu: File > New Project, Open Project, Recent Projects
- Menu: Tools > Change History, Compare ROMs
- Status bar: Show current project name
- Table browser: Icon/color for modified tables

### Change History Window


### Comment Dialog (when saving changes)


## Example Use Case

**Scenario:** User is tuning their NC Miata

1. Create project: 'NC1 Stage 1 Tune'
2. Add ROMs:
   - 'Stock' (baseline)
   - 'Working Tune' (current edits)
3. Edit fuel table in 'Working Tune'
4. App prompts: 'Add comment?' → 'Richened WOT for safety'
5. Later: View change history → see all modifications with comments
6. Compare 'Working Tune' to 'Stock' → see all differences
7. Export change log → PDF report for dyno shop

## Dependencies
- Requires table editing functionality (nc-rom-editor-3in.6)
- Nice-to-have: ROM comparison tool (nc-rom-editor-3in.8)

## Benefits
- No more Excel spreadsheets
- Complete audit trail of changes
- Professional documentation
- Easy comparison of tune versions
- Safer tuning (can revert changes)
- Share change logs with tuners/shops

title: Build NC Miata ROM Editor - Core functionality
Add error handling and validation
description:
Add validation logic for ROM files:

INFRASTRUCTURE COMPLETE (via nc-rom-editor-013):
✓ Custom exception classes exist (RomFileError, InvalidRomFileError, etc.)
✓ Logging framework in place
✓ Error handling throughout codebase
✓ Meaningful error messages shown to users

REMAINING VALIDATION LOGIC:
- Validate ROM file structure (size, format, headers)
- Detect corrupt or malformed ROM files
- Validate checksums before reading (if available)
- Validate table addresses are within ROM bounds
- Validate scaling expressions are safe/valid
- Add file integrity checks (magic numbers, expected patterns)
- Handle edge cases (truncated files, invalid data types)
- Add unit tests for validation logic

title: Create packaging for Windows distribution
description: Use PyInstaller to package as standalone .exe for Windows. Test on clean Windows system.

title: Implement ROM ID detection and XML matching
description: Read ROM ID from binary file and automatically find/load the matching XML definition file from metadata/ directory. Remove hardcoded definition path.

title: Implement ROM binary file I/O
description: Read and write NC Miata ECU ROM binary files (.bin). Handle file opening, saving, and basic validation.
notes: Completed: ROM definition parser, ROM binary reader with scaling, table browser/viewer UI. Working viewer loads and displays tables. Identified issue: hardcoded definition must be replaced with ROM ID detection.

title: Parse RomDrop metadata/definition files
description: Parse XML definition files from RomDrop that describe ROM structure, table locations, data types, and conversions.
notes: Created ROM definition parser that successfully loads lf9veb.xml with 511 tables and 838 scalings

title: Build table viewer UI component
description: Create PySide6 UI components to display 2D and 3D calibration tables (fuel maps, ignition timing, etc.) with proper visualization.
notes: Created table browser and viewer widgets with full ROM definition integration

title: Implement table editing operations
description: Add/subtract values from cells, interpolate values, copy/paste table data, undo/redo functionality.

title: Implement checksum calculation and validation
description: Calculate and verify ROM checksums. Auto-recalculate when saving modified ROM files.

title: Build ROM comparison tool
description: Compare two ROM files (e.g., stock vs modified) and highlight differences in tables and raw data.

title: Implement import/export functionality
description: Export individual tables or full ROM files. Import tables from other ROMs.

title: Polish UI for professional look and feel
description:
Improve the UI polish to make the application feel professional and modern. Qt is capable of beautiful UIs but current implementation is functional MVP without visual polish.

**Visual Styling:**
- Add modern stylesheet with proper color scheme (dark/light theme support)
- Improve typography (fonts, sizes, weights)
- Add proper spacing, padding, and margins throughout
- Add visual hierarchy (headers, sections, grouping)
- Style tables with alternating row colors
- Add hover effects and visual feedback
- Polish buttons, inputs, and controls

**Icons & Graphics:**
- Add icons to menu items (File > Open, Save, etc.)
- Add icons to tree view items (categories, table types)
- Add toolbar with icon buttons for common actions
- Add status bar icons for feedback
- Consider custom window icon/logo

**User Experience:**
- Add keyboard shortcuts (Ctrl+O, Ctrl+S, Ctrl+Q, Ctrl+F, etc.)
- Add tooltips to all interactive elements
- Add context menus (right-click) for tables
- Add loading indicators/progress bars for long operations
- Improve status messages with better formatting
- Add search box for table filtering (complements nc-rom-editor-jf2)

**Layout Improvements:**
- Better splitter ratios and remember user preferences
- Add proper minimum sizes for widgets
- Responsive layout that works at different window sizes
- Add scrollbars where needed
- Improve table headers and column sizing

**Data Display:**
- Color-code table cells by value (heatmap for 3D tables)
- Add formatting options for numbers (decimals, scientific notation)
- Highlight modified cells
- Add visual indicators for out-of-range values

**Modern Features:**
- Consider tabbed interface for multiple ROMs
- Add recent files menu
- Add preferences/settings dialog
- Add about dialog with version info and credits

**Reference:**
Look at professional Qt apps like VLC, OBS Studio, or Qt Creator for inspiration. The framework is capable of beautiful UIs - we just need to use its features.

title: Add filter/search to table browser
description:
Add search/filter functionality to the table tree (left panel) to filter tables by:
- Category/group name
- Table name
Should support real-time filtering as user types.
close_reason:
Implemented table browser search/filter feature with:
- Search box with real-time filtering
- Filters by table name, type, address, and category
- Keyboard shortcut (Ctrl+F) to focus search
- Clear button to reset filter
- Auto-expands matching categories
- Case-insensitive search
